methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Gallox Semiconductors is a seed-stage chip design company founded by Jon McCandless and Debdeep Jena that has raised $5M+. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

Given Gallox's early stage and limited public disclosure, only the Stage-Progression method is reliably applicable. Using total capital raised ($5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $12.5M. A Peer-Median approach cannot be applied: while 10 same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-filter matches (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier) preclude a statistically meaningful median. Relying on all-sector medians would obscure company-specific risk.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. This reflects a modest step-up from deployed capital consistent with seed-stage risk and current traction. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to better-capitalized competitors, path to Series A fundraising, and sector-wide macro headwinds affecting semiconductor venture funding. A peer-based cross-check remains infeasible without more granular data on comparable seed-stage valuations in frontier chip design.
